site.name: MindGuard
site.description: MindGuard is a quiet place to understand what you're feeling, try a grounding exercise, and find real people to talk to — no account, no judgment, no diagnosis.
site.disclaimer: MindGuard provides general educational information and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of a qualified provider with questions about a mental health condition.

crisisStrip.text: In immediate danger or crisis? Call 112 (India emergency) or the helplines below — you don't have to go through this alone.

hero.headline: Your mind deserves the same care as everything else you look after.
hero.lede: MindGuard is a quiet place to understand what you're feeling, try a grounding exercise, and find real people to talk to — no account, no judgment, no diagnosis.
hero.note: This site offers information and self-care tools — it is not a substitute for professional care.

topics.name: Anxiety
topics.description: Persistent worry, racing thoughts, or physical tension that doesn't match the moment.
points: Restlessness, a racing heart, or trouble concentrating
Worry that feels hard to switch off, even when things are okay
Avoiding situations because of how they might make you feel
Grounding and breathing exercises can help in the moment

topics.name: Low Mood & Depression
topics.description: A heaviness or flatness that lingers, and makes ordinary things feel harder.
points: Losing interest in things you used to enjoy
Feeling tired even after rest, or sleeping too much or too little
Difficulty concentrating or making decisions
If this lasts more than two weeks, it's worth talking to someone

topics.name: Stress & Burnout
topics.description: Feeling stretched too thin for too long, at work, school, or home.
points: Feeling cynical, detached, or numb about things that used to matter
Physical symptoms — headaches, tight shoulders, trouble sleeping
A drop in the sense that your effort is making a difference
Small, regular breaks help more than one long one

topics.name: Sleep Difficulties
topics.description: Trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up rested.
points: Racing thoughts right when you try to fall asleep
Waking up frequently through the night
Sleep and mood affect each other in both directions
A consistent wind-down routine can help more than it seems

topics.name: Grief & Loss
topics.description: The process of adjusting to losing someone or something important.
points: Grief doesn't move in a straight line — it can come in waves
There's no fixed timeline for how long it should take
It's common to feel guilt, anger, or relief alongside sadness
Talking about the person or thing you lost is usually helpful, not harmful

topics.name: Loneliness
topics.description: Feeling disconnected, even when you're around other people.
points: Loneliness is about connection quality, not headcount
It can affect sleep, mood, and even physical health over time
Small, low-pressure contact often rebuilds connection faster than big plans
Peer support groups and helplines exist specifically for this

moodCheckIn.title: How are you, right now?
moodCheckIn.subtitle: A short check-in — naming a feeling can take some of its weight off.
moodCheckIn.notePlaceholder: What's going on, if you want to note it (optional)

grounding.title: 5-4-3-2-1 grounding
grounding.subtitle: A quick way to steady yourself when things feel like too much.

steps.title: 5 things you can see
steps.description: Look around and name five things you can see right now.

steps.title: 4 things you can touch
steps.description: Notice four things you can physically feel — the floor, your clothes, the air.

steps.title: 3 things you can hear
steps.description: Listen for three distinct sounds, near or far.

steps.title: 2 things you can smell
steps.description: Notice two scents, or think of two you like if none are present.

steps.title: 1 thing you can taste
steps.description: Notice one taste in your mouth, or take a sip of something.

supportDirectory.name: Tele-MANAS
supportDirectory.description: Government of India national mental health helpline, multiple languages.

supportDirectory.name: Vandrevala Foundation
supportDirectory.description: Free, confidential crisis support by phone, chat, and WhatsApp.

supportDirectory.name: iCall (TISS)
supportDirectory.description: Psychosocial helpline offering counselling by trained professionals.

supportDirectory.name: AASRA
supportDirectory.description: Crisis intervention for those feeling distressed, lonely, or suicidal.

supportDirectory.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
supportDirectory.description: Call or text for free, confidential crisis support.

supportDirectory.name: Samaritans
supportDirectory.description: Free, confidential emotional support for anyone struggling to cope.

emergencyNote: If you or someone else is in immediate physical danger, call your local emergency number right away (112 in India, 911 in the US, 999 in the UK) or go to the nearest emergency room.

about.headline: Awareness first, stigma last.
paragraphs: Most people wait years before talking to anyone about what they're carrying. MindGuard exists to close that gap a little — with plain-language information, tools you can use in under two minutes, and a clear path to real support.
We don't diagnose, sell anything, or track you. This page works the same with or without an internet connection, on purpose.

stats.label: People experience a mental health condition

newsletter.headline: One gentle reminder, monthly.
newsletter.subtitle: A short note with one self-care idea and one thing worth knowing. No spam, unsubscribe any time.

footer.note: © 2026 MindGuard. Informational only — not a substitute for professional care.
